textByHash.01f5993f36e67d7ec442ea2c66c3859fa41df13a: My fez? I got it from Ali Morrisane, it's a uniform of sorts, apparently it makes us more visible, but I'm not too sure about it.
textByHash.0624e7512df4375452dcac3475cb1e7d2f8c211b: Do you think I'm mad? Do you think that Ali Morrisane would throw his magic carpet monopoly away?
textByHash.07a1ce66c5bbf0d89280ef447cfc07cd7383aa22: I would be really grateful if you could get that monkey off my back.
textByHash.0ba9dc9c895034c9c3b11a600e7d7f379c399eef: You know those small little guys, not the dwarves now mind.
textByHash.0e9a5c12c241cc9a0a8261e53d5830067fdbfba3: Well besides the obvious - looking after this station, I'm trying to figure out how these Tentis manage to cultivate such delicious pineapples.
textByHash.0fe84561cf33e7a7fbbbb552f893e3e893fca9c1: Well I'd preferred to have been running one of the carpet stations at a hub such as Pollnivneach. I was a bit slow off the mark to get that gig though. Still business in Nardah isn't bad for a terminal. At least
textByHash.1173d70688e72b3eee4264bc85eae1c27199ff71: From here you can travel to the Shantay Pass.
textByHash.12c0148d683840eba864a279aa3defbb642e56e9: There's never a dull moment around that man, he's always looking for a way to make a quick coin or two.
textByHash.1a0f56383136ee06bfc9003a4bf70503a30b670d: Ali Morrisane's carpet fleet flies in any weather, though! Do you require our services?
textByHash.1cbcbfddc78d009f82303b4680be92e74cf75bdd: It's quite simple really, Ali Morrisane has hired myself and a few others to set up carpet stations at some of the desert's more populated places and run flights between the stations.
textByHash.21449e9cb113687723500c198b12ef5bfe17bd41: Well you for one, and besides that we get quite a few archaeologists from the dig site passing through to examine the golem.
textByHash.2b66abf40d064117c1d11717c1231789e132613e: He's his own monkey, he does whatever suits him, a total nuisance.
textByHash.371e5876644af13efc14d902245be0ddd0031909: Not too bad, the hubs are generally quite busy. But the stations in Uzer and the Bedabin camp could do with a bit more traffic.
textByHash.3ac2563d63c1c1f3981094198df80a4b8ff7a822: Not a hope. Could you imagine the mess there'd be if people were constantly zooming through Al Kharid and Pollnivneach? It would be chaos. This way, we can keep the carpet traffic outside towns and other busy places.
textByHash.3db653af6667154cdbf3f59d28f4d17d5cf271a8: The carpets here will take you to the south of Pollnivneach. Do you want to take a lift?
textByHash.4010b6b5cda89e7303b7b4686eb04cf098f04323: Maybe once Menaphos opens her gates again, the station will make a fortune once more.
textByHash.4339d5a999558a441121ea455d72efeb69b6341c: Greetings, desert traveller. Do you require the services of Ali Morrisane's flying carpet fleet?
textByHash.44456dc43eadd1f10788f6b53bfa7a3fba406be5: I don't think Ali is prepared to take on Gnome Air just yet, their gliders are much faster than our carpets, besides that I think we are in the short haul business, something that would only work in harsh conditions like
textByHash.4a69eb22f7d76e8f7388ebbe7839e97f2e3be990: Anyway I think that Ali M's setup here will prove really successful and maybe once we're properly established we could try compete with those gnomes.
textByHash.4a9e241ad9cacb44e7b42c783295257606c40871: Not really. The only reason I have come up with to date is that by keeping the station open, people become familiar with it.
textByHash.4e0380b2d2db035595d161984bdf7fdd8d5c2ebf: The latest idea from the great Ali Morrisane. Desert travel will never be the same again.
textByHash.57620a2243324af7c17764bb6a6a7f19fec6a178: I work here renting out magic carpets. I'm from Pollnivneach so it is a handy job, I don't have to commute too far to work every day.
textByHash.5a956886a191e7ec25f8e9c080a16967773dd7a6: Well it doesn't keep the sun out of my eyes and after a while sitting out in the desert they really begin to burn.
textByHash.5cbf7600d53b717c3a8b4fd1567d909c352670ee: I'm not really too sure, it's just an enchanted rug really, made out of special Ugthanki hair. It flies to whatever destination its owner commands.
textByHash.5ceb8e0ac7e1da86f4f536c0b34cd04d8072b8e2: I'll try to help you as much as I can.
textByHash.846f45b5bb30dd1b95bf9bab1defe55251072953: Right now I have to fill my day trying to come up with reasons why Ali M should keep this place open.
textByHash.8798a0711e764dc72648c819123bb68d2974e483: You know, Sophanem, city of the dead and all that?
textByHash.8b7f1bfedaf50e9e5a437a4778041bbe75bf9cb2: Just think of the friction burns you would get if you were in a carpet crash.
textByHash.8d360070bf57d88542bbd6832f7fd19674c29b80: Until recently I was looking after one of the busiest carpet stations. But that all changed since Menaphos closed its gates.
textByHash.915d734e8e2e9150dddb3b7d08266121bc2e3604: Good heavens! I've never seen a weather like this before! It looks like a real sandstorm is coming up!
textByHash.92a7dc5c3a982a1357078c54de5dc33f4fbecb99: You can travel from here back to the Shantay Pass.
textByHash.95fe71415a9ef21febcc1d50ce34ec12e9abfb34: The second major carpet hub station, to the south of Pollnivneach is in easy walking distance from there.
textByHash.9781d1fcf5a38fcc8cebbcb1fb76f22465a6e4db: I don't dislike monkeys, it's just that monkey. I don't know, I might just be paranoid but I think he's... well... evil.
textByHash.9d6a5d4adb5acfc14b35b884f46a352e792e32b1: No it's been a quite a while since I last saw him.
textByHash.9edb381f7deff3630d9855ec6fe697e5f1cb416e: I suppose because people would just walk. Getting lost isn't too much of a problem generally, but it's a different matter when you're in the middle of the Kharidian desert with a dry waterskin and no idea
textByHash.9f44160fddbadcf928e012b724f4c79121d33d3e: I look after the carpet station here. The place is a bit dead though. Ha! I'm just too much.
textByHash.a1146f75c98ad739a8a89477c299c097908d33b6: Well Pollnivneach is the ideal location for setting up a carpet station.
textByHash.a3f78fdb7386b41004b86a3e3b7baa83f67c2a36: Well they have already established an Airline, Gnome Air...
textByHash.a5558ec08d48506ec6d7882d7d565675e9820ac8: From here you can travel to the Shantay Pass - the Southern gate of Al Kharid.
textByHash.a700bc2c4da687468b55759b1bd412da853f6eb8: The carpet or the whole enterprise?
textByHash.aa6f47599d7fe4915322f798c30b79557b141745: I shouldn't say this really, but sometimes I begin to question some of Ali Morrisane's ideas, he says that associating a monkey with any product will increase sales. I just don't know, what will be next?
textByHash.b00a4ee3ff9908be03917b3bf760dc78a67c1845: Well this is a good position for desert traffic. Shantay seems to have a nice little money spinner setup, but I reckon, this could turn out even better.
textByHash.b5a751478df00fd380f3790905afea1166b276bc: What, you haven't heard of Ali M? Possibly the greatest salesman of the Kharidian empire if not all Gielinor?
textByHash.b8e256465c0099590b7e8e2d46eae4336ef42c72: From here you can travel to Uzer, to the Bedabin camp or to the North of Pollnivneach.
textByHash.bab90e540244d2b0ac8cd7d0935d942cb7789fb7: I doubt it, amphibians don't have the same cutesy factor as monkeys.
textByHash.c1c54f65f89a6e407db6b67ba7d5e90ff4e692ce: Do you like it? I haven't really made my mind up about it yet. You see it's not all that practical for desert conditions.
textByHash.c288add31fb4bd0406bf3bbfff45f61713bba5c0: I wouldn't be surprised, he's mentioned to me on a few occasions that he is thinking of diversifying even further.
textByHash.c5730e2b6ecd3de651aaa3d8bf0572cc9e985ead: Aw come on, the joke wasn't that bad.
textByHash.c605a858b8f1a51eed868b231fab93be1d01684e: From here you can travel to Nardah and the Menaphite cities of Sophanem and Menaphos.
textByHash.cc4fb29b4386bed0aa68a8e5fb12fdb47f993873: You know something, I reckon that he's trying to take on those gnomes at their own game and I'd bet good money that he'll probably win.
textByHash.ce901da7f2d9b8f14bc51404db0c5575a1ae5857: You mightn't realise it, but this is quite a busy station.
textByHash.d336d34f2daeaf997d2094eafa8ad335fc20abe4: You see it's located halfway between Al Kharid, and the Menaphite cities and close enough to Nardah too, so we get more than enough traffic to keep the business running.
textByHash.e2a5440472cde353dbbe9efe28ad1016649f84aa: people come here for the bank and to see the herbalist.
textByHash.ef3670725edf764c2e8914182685992bb4ead684: The carpets here will take you to the south of Pollnivneach.
textByHash.f6bda791f033a6c581c6b0368fdeff2b47436781: Fair enough, magic carpet travel isn't for everyone. Enjoy the walk.
textByHash.f817187c93758f081fac2224f0eab4aaddf9bb72: Ha! No doubt old Ali M instigated the whole thing.
textByHash.fd5a25455d4b2c007f2129316fde793af4644589: Not the most remarkable of names, not that it matters, you see everyone in town knows me as Flash.
textByHash.fdfac76df4ce702f82abdecb2b78c68d0febea2a: Would you? It sometimes gives me these stares... ~A visible shiver runs down his back.
